pi_items.source: Analysis of the Clustered motors.ork file shows it uses a '4-ring' cluster configuration, which physically defines exactly 4 outboard motors and no central motor.
Removing one motor from the actual 4-motor base rocket leaves 3 motors, contradicting the metadata's 6. (Note: verifier.py still expects exactly 6).

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on a real OpenRocket example file 'Clustered motors.ork' downloaded from the official repository. There is a fundamental contradiction in the task design: the metadata and instructions claim a '7-motor clustered rocket' and expect a failure state of 6 motors. However, empirical code analysis of the base .ork file confirms it uses a '4-ring' cluster configuration, which contains exactly 4 motors. Consequently, removing one motor logically leaves 3 motors, making the verifier's strict expectation of 6 <motor> tags impossible to satisfy unless the agent arbitrarily alters the design to inject more motors than visually present.
